pr.title: Fix Devin session CSV export dropping skill proposal summary
pr.description:
## Summary

Devin Review on PR #126 (now merged) flagged a real data-loss bug in `devin_session_import._generate_export_csv`: the `skill_proposal` rows put `skill_name` into the `summary` column and never emitted `proposal_summary` at all, so the summary was silently dropped from CSV exports and `skill_name` was mislabeled under the `summary` header.

Fix matches the representation already used for skill-proposal timeline events (`devin_session_import.py:842`):

```diff
- p.get("skill_name", ""),          # column = "summary" header → wrong field, summary lost
+ f"{p.get('skill_name', '')}: {p.get('proposal_summary', '')}",
```

This keeps `skill_name` visible while ensuring the `summary` column actually carries `proposal_summary`, with zero CSV schema changes (action/artifact rows untouched). Extended `test_export_csv_rows` to assert the skill_proposal `summary` column contains both the proposal summary and skill name.

## Validation
- `poetry run pytest tests/test_devin_session_import.py -q` → 63 passed
- `poetry run pytest -q` → 4264 passed, 1 skipped
- `poetry run ruff check src/ tests/` → 0 errors

## What did not change
CSV header/columns, action & artifact rows, JSON/Markdown exports, and all other behavior. Revit 2024 baseline unaffected.

Link to Devin session: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/8ad1c9ac0d2f4252bb77647f1eabaac7
Requested by: @plamen-dev
pr.author: plamen-dev

commits.author: plamen.hristov@imsdevlab.onmicrosoft.com
commits.message:
Fix Devin session CSV export dropping skill proposal summary

Devin Review (PR #126) found that the skill_proposal rows in
_generate_export_csv placed skill_name into the 'summary' column and
omitted proposal_summary entirely, silently losing it from CSV exports.
Use the same '{skill_name}: {proposal_summary}' representation already
used for timeline events so the summary column carries the actual
summary without losing skill_name.

Co-Authored-By: Devin AI <158243242+devin-ai-integration[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
